jokes.text: Larry wearing the exact same outfit as yesterday and justifying it by saying he didn't feel like going through the decision process
jokes.setup: Cheryl notices Larry is wearing the same jacket as yesterday
jokes.punchline: Larry's lazy justification that he didn't want to go through the decision process
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's character - his laziness and ability to rationalize anything. Relatable but distinctly Larry.

jokes.text: Cheryl suggesting Larry's maroon jacket should go to the homeless guy at the gas station
jokes.setup: Cheryl criticizing Larry's maroon jacket
jokes.punchline: Her suggestion to give it away to a homeless person
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the callback and shows the couple's dynamic. Efficient comedy.

jokes.text: Larry's passionate defense: 'This is my show night jacket. I wore this on show night.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl trying to get rid of his jacket
jokes.punchline: Larry's reverent attachment to this awful jacket because of its Seinfeld history
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry's nostalgia and attachment to meaningless things. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Larry's response to learning about the Jewish groom: 'Really? So am I. They make the best husbands.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentions her sister is marrying a Jew
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-congratulatory comment about Jewish husbands
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-promotion with ironic undertones given his actual track record as a husband.

jokes.text: Richard Lewis's outgoing message theft accusation and his elaborate Paris backstory
jokes.setup: Richard claims Larry stole his answering machine message
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific claim about writing it in Paris seven years ago
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard Lewis neurosis - making a mundane thing into an epic grievance with unnecessary details.

jokes.text: Richard's reliability argument: 'Whose word are you gonna trust, okay? An ex-alcoholic's, or a person who was lucid 24 hours a day his whole life?'
jokes.setup: Larry and Richard arguing over who had the message first
jokes.punchline: Richard using his sobriety status as evidence of reliability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware comedy where Richard turns his addiction history into a credibility argument. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Richard's pathetic appeal: 'I don't have a wife, I don't have... My parents are dead.'
jokes.setup: Richard trying to get Larry to change the message
jokes.punchline: Using his loneliness and dead parents as emotional leverage
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Richard Lewis - weaponizing his tragic circumstances for petty victories.

jokes.text: Larry's mock-sympathetic repetition: 'He doesn't have a wife, and his parents are dead.'
jokes.setup: Richard's pathetic appeal
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately parroting it back with mock sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Larry's mock sympathy before he caves to Richard's manipulation.

jokes.text: The new answering machine message: 'Hi, we're not in. Please leave a message at the tone. Thank you.'
jokes.setup: Larry changing his message after Richard's complaint
jokes.punchline: The completely generic, personality-free replacement message
jokes.explanation: The anticlimax is the joke - after all that drama, Larry goes to the most boring possible message.

jokes.text: You're a small, petty man.
jokes.setup: Larry calling himself 'a big, expansive man'
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling Richard small and petty
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete lack of self-awareness in the same breath

jokes.text: Larry's baptism ignorance: 'What is a baptism anyway?' 'I have no idea. Something to do with water?'
jokes.setup: Larry needs to know what to wear to the baptism
jokes.punchline: His complete ignorance of basic Christian ritual
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the climax and perfectly shows Larry's cultural isolation.

jokes.text: Larry asking what 'the average gentile' wears to a baptism
jokes.setup: Larry trying to figure out baptism attire
jokes.punchline: His clinical, anthropological approach to gentile culture
jokes.explanation: Great phrase - 'average gentile' - shows Larry treating Christianity like a foreign culture to study.

jokes.text: Larry's conversion observation: 'You guys come to our side, we don't go to your side. Yeah. Jews don't convert.'
jokes.setup: Discussing why the groom is converting
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact observation about conversion patterns
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with Larry's characteristic blunt cultural commentary.

jokes.text: Larry's lobster evangelism analogy: 'It's like, I like lobster. Do I go around pushing lobster on people?'
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about Christian proselytizing
jokes.punchline: Comparing religious conversion to lobster evangelism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist analogy that perfectly captures Larry's perspective. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Larry's extended lobster missionary work: 'You go to Africa, you travel all over the world. Eat lobster, have some more lobster, it's good. We want you to have lobster!'
jokes.setup: The lobster evangelism analogy
jokes.punchline: Larry escalating it to global lobster missionary work
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd analogy, though slightly less punchy than the initial setup.

jokes.text: Larry's paranoid ticket theft theory about the 'creepy guy' watching him enter the security code
jokes.setup: Larry can't find the airline tickets
jokes.punchline: His elaborate conspiracy theory about office break-in for Monterey tickets
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry paranoia - turning a simple misplacement into an elaborate crime scenario.

jokes.text: Cheryl's logic: 'Why would somebody steal tickets to Monterey?' Larry: 'Why not? Monterey's a beautiful place.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl questioning Larry's theft theory
jokes.punchline: Larry's tourist-board-like defense of Monterey as theft-worthy
jokes.explanation: Good back-and-forth showing Larry's commitment to his absurd theory.

jokes.text: Cheryl's sarcastic observation: 'Yeah, parking is hard to find at the airport. That's something new, Larry.'
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about no parking spaces
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's dry sarcasm about this obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm about an obvious situation. Functional but not special.

jokes.text: Larry wondering if the ticket thief 'brought a date' to their flight
jokes.setup: Larry still convinced someone stole their tickets
jokes.punchline: Wondering about the thief's social plans
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail that shows Larry's mind continuing to build the conspiracy.

jokes.text: Larry's passive-aggressive commentary to the slow parker: 'Yeah, it's all right, take your time. Nobody's waiting for you. Make a call, have a cup of coffee. And then have a nice bagel. Take a little nap.'
jokes.setup: Someone taking too long to leave a parking space
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalating sarcastic suggestions for time-wasting activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry passive-aggression with good escalation. The bagel and nap details are nicely absurd.

jokes.text: The parking guy's dismissive response: 'Get here earlier. It's not my fault.'
jokes.setup: Larry's passive-aggressive commentary
jokes.punchline: The guy's completely reasonable but unhelpful response
jokes.explanation: Good reality check response that deflates Larry's indignation with simple logic.

jokes.text: Larry's complaint about parking lot logic: 'If the lot's full, why do they even let you go in? They shouldn't even give you a ticket.'
jokes.setup: Having to circle a full parking lot
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but impractical solution
jokes.explanation: Solid Larry logic complaint - technically correct but missing the business reality.

jokes.text: Larry confronting the wrong person about ticket theft and the man's response: 'See, if this were yours, it would say Fucking Douchebag.'
jokes.setup: Larry convinced he found the ticket thief
jokes.punchline: The man's brutal comeback when showing his legitimate ticket
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and comeback. The guy perfectly reads Larry and delivers a memorable insult.

jokes.text: Larry's follow-up: 'Sorry about your mother.'
jokes.setup: Just got brutally insulted by someone whose mother died
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward attempt at politeness after the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - trying to be decent after completely embarrassing himself.

jokes.text: Second ticket theft confrontation ending with 'Go fuck yourself.'
jokes.setup: Larry accusing another innocent person
jokes.punchline: Even more direct dismissal of Larry's paranoia
jokes.explanation: Escalates the pattern but less creative than the first confrontation.

jokes.text: Larry's random grape observation: 'Have you ever had a fresh grape? I've had fresh apples, never had a fresh grape.'
jokes.setup: Driving through farm country
jokes.punchline: Larry's bizarre contemplation of fruit freshness hierarchies
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Larry observation that somehow makes you think. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Larry's fruit ignorance: 'Never seen a pear. Never saw a pear outside of a fruit stand.'
jokes.setup: Continuing fruit observations
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission of profound agricultural ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Larry's complete disconnection from nature.

jokes.text: Larry's random observations: 'Grape works as a soda, sort of as a gum, but I wonder why it doesn't work as a pie.'
jokes.setup: Random food thoughts while driving
jokes.punchline: Larry's analysis of grape's commercial applications
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry random observation that's actually kind of thoughtful in its absurdity.

jokes.text: grape works as a soda, sort of as a gum... but I wonder why it doesn't work as a pie.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing his random observations
jokes.punchline: His analytical approach to grape product marketing
jokes.explanation: Larry's brain treating random thoughts like important consumer insights

jokes.text: Larry's America complaint: 'Did we really need Alaska and Hawaii? They gotta ruin everything. They ruined the continental United States.'
jokes.setup: Random thoughts while driving
jokes.punchline: Larry's aesthetic objection to non-contiguous states
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Larry complaint about something completely beyond anyone's control. Very quotable.

jokes.text: did we think we really needed Alaska and Hawaii? They gotta ruin everything.
jokes.setup: Larry's stream-of-consciousness while driving
jokes.punchline: Blaming Alaska and Hawaii for ruining America
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd political commentary that could only come from Larry

jokes.text: Larry's geographic purism: 'You have a beautiful Pacific coast, Atlantic coast, that's the continental United States. You don't need more states. We're not the British empire.'
jokes.setup: Complaint about Alaska and Hawaii
jokes.punchline: Larry's passionate defense of geographic minimalism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the British empire comparison showing Larry's weird nationalism.

jokes.text: Larry jumping into baptismal water screaming 'Stop! No, Larry!'
jokes.setup: Larry witnessing his first baptism
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding leads him to 'rescue' the groom
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and payoff. Larry's ignorance leads to the ultimate cultural misunderstanding. Physically and emotionally climactic.

jokes.text: The aftermath reaction: 'You happy about this, Larry? Is this what you had in mind? You like this, you dick?'
jokes.setup: Larry has ruined the baptism
jokes.punchline: Becky's furious accusation that he did it on purpose
jokes.explanation: Good angry reaction that shows everyone thinks Larry did this intentionally.

jokes.text: I thought that he was drowning him. I'm sorry!
jokes.setup: Everyone angry at Larry for interrupting
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent explanation reveals his cultural ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Larry's ignorance causing disaster

jokes.text: Becky's accusation: 'You didn't want to lose a Jew. You didn't want him to convert.'
jokes.setup: Larry's baptism disruption
jokes.punchline: The assumption that Larry was protecting Jewish membership
jokes.explanation: Smart line that gives Larry a motivation he didn't have but that makes perfect sense to everyone.

jokes.text: Larry's defense: 'I don't care if I lose Jews. Take them all, I don't need them.'
jokes.setup: Accused of protecting Jewish converts
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual dismissal of his own people
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Larry - in trying to defend himself, he says something equally offensive in the other direction.

jokes.text: Dave Levin's Jewish conspiracy interpretation: 'What you did is a very gutsy thing. As one Jew to another, it's a gutsy thing to come in and step in. A mitzvah for my family.'
jokes.setup: Larry accidentally disrupted the baptism
jokes.punchline: Dave treating it as intentional Jewish activism
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Larry gets credit for something he didn't do while everyone misunderstands his motives.

jokes.text: Baptism technical requirements: 'You just kind of started it, but it didn't really work. You need to be dunked three times. You need two more dunks.'
jokes.setup: Trying to complete the interrupted baptism
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic requirements for proper baptism
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist bureaucracy humor - treating baptism like a technical procedure.

jokes.text: Sheldon's spiritual revelation: 'When I was held under the water, I felt a revelation like it was some sort of message from God that I shouldn't do this.'
jokes.setup: Trying to complete the baptism
jokes.punchline: The baptism convinced him NOT to convert
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic twist - the baptism works in reverse, convincing him to stay Jewish. Great story structure.

jokes.text: Sheldon's conclusion: 'After I came out of the water, I didn't feel like a Christian, I felt like even more of a Jew.'
jokes.setup: His baptismal revelation
jokes.punchline: The ritual made him more Jewish instead of Christian
jokes.explanation: Excellent follow-up that completes the ironic reversal. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: Becky's final accusation: 'We're not getting married! You happy now, you son of a bitch?'
jokes.setup: Sheldon refuses to convert after the baptism
jokes.punchline: The wedding is called off and Larry gets blamed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the full consequences of Larry's innocent mistake.

jokes.text: Cheryl's final line: 'We don't need you.' and Christian response: 'Everything's a big problem with you people!'
jokes.setup: Argument about conversion and recruitment
jokes.punchline: The conversation devolving into ethnic stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Shows how Larry's innocent mistake escalated into full cultural warfare.

jokes.text: The jacket callback: Larry seeing the homeless man wearing his maroon jacket
jokes.setup: Cheryl gave Larry's jacket to homeless man at beginning
jokes.punchline: Seeing it again after their disaster trip
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that bookends the episode nicely, though not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: There's my jacket, there's the guy you gave my jacket to!
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl arriving home
jokes.punchline: Seeing the homeless man wearing Larry's beloved jacket
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual callback to the episode's opening conflict

jokes.text: The airline tickets resolution: Homeless man found their tickets and tried to call them
jokes.setup: Larry's paranoid theories about ticket theft
jokes.punchline: The tickets were simply lost and found by someone trying to help
jokes.explanation: Good ironic resolution that shows Larry's paranoia was completely wrong. Nice story structure.
